macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Advance Led by Tech; S&P 500 Up 0.8%, Nasdaq 1.3%, Dow 0.6%
items.summary: U.S. markets rose for a second day with the S&P 500 closing at 6,946.13, Nasdaq at 23,152.08, and Dow at 49,482.15, driven by easing AI sector concerns and strong tech performances including Microsoft up 3% and Palantir up 4.2%. Nvidia beat earnings estimates post-close with 75% data center growth but showed little change, while Salesforce slid 4% on weak guidance.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Beat Expectations but Markets Show Mixed Reaction
items.summary: Nvidia reported better-than-expected Q4 earnings and 75% data center growth after market close, stabilizing digital assets like Bitcoin at $68,176 and Ethereum at $2,063. Early Thursday trading saw Nasdaq reject 25,500 resistance and retreat toward 24,881 support amid tech selloff signals.

items.title: S&P 500 Trapped in Narrowest Trading Range Since 2018 Amid Sector Divergence
items.summary: The S&P 500 remains in an extremely narrow trading range over the past 30 sessions, up just 1.4% year-to-date, with high dispersion between sectors: energy, materials, and staples leading gains while tech faces potential inflection. U.S. stocks mixed early Thursday, teetering on weekly loss after volatile open.

items.title: Tech Tumbles Post-Nvidia; Nasdaq Tests Key Supports, Dow Flat
items.summary: Nasdaq dropped nearly 1.5% retesting 25,000 and 24,881 support after rejecting 25,500, while Dow turned lower failing 50,000 and S&P 500 formed double-top near oversold levels. Volatility persists with oil rising and focus on US data and trade policy.

items.title: Circle Internet Jumps 35.5% on Strong Results; HSBC Up 7.9%
items.summary: Circle Internet Group surged 35.5% after beating expectations on stablecoin demand; HSBC rose 7.9% on strong 2025 results and upbeat outlook. European stocks hit records on tariff relief, with ASML up 2% on AI cycle focus.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: STS Digital Raises $30 Million from CMT Digital and Kraken Amid Crypto Downturn
items.summary: Bermuda-based crypto trading platform STS Digital secured $30 million in funding from CMT Digital and Kraken to expand options contracts on over 400 cryptocurrencies, targeting institutional investors despite retail market exodus due to plummeting prices.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: DataJoint Launches Agentic AI Control Layer for Scientific Workflows
items.summary: DataJoint announced DataJoint Agentic AI, a governed execution layer that enables semi-autonomous AI operation on structured, provenance-rich data for scientific research. The platform captures multi-modal scientific data with embedded metadata and computational provenance, enabling reproducible and defensible AI-driven insights in regulated R&D environments.

items.title: MIT Researchers Develop Method to Double LLM Training Speed
items.summary: Researchers from MIT developed a technique that leverages computational downtime to accelerate reasoning LLM training by 70-210% without additional overhead. The method trains a smaller model to predict outputs of larger reasoning models, reducing computational workload while preserving accuracy.

items.title: Xanadu and Lockheed Martin Launch Quantum Machine Learning Research Initiative
items.summary: Xanadu and Lockheed Martin announced a joint research initiative to advance quantum machine learning theory and applications, focusing on generative models and Fourier-based operations. The collaboration aims to explore how quantum computers can address limitations of classical machine learning in data-scarce applications.

items.title: University of Waterloo Study Proposes Framework for Training Wise AI Systems
items.summary: An international research team led by the University of Waterloo published the first study proposing realistic methods to integrate wisdom into large language models. The approach focuses on teaching AI metacognition, intellectual humility, perspective-seeking, and context adaptation to create more robust and safer AI systems.

items.title: Machine Learning Model Detects Fraudulent Research Papers at Scale
items.summary: A BMJ-published study developed a machine learning model to identify cancer research papers from 'paper mills' that produce fraudulent manuscripts. The model found nearly 10% of cancer papers published between 1999-2024 showed signs of paper mill origins, highlighting AI's dual role in detecting and potentially enabling research fraud.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie Returns with Marathon Amid Streaming Controversy
items.summary: Bungie is set to release its highly anticipated extraction shooter Marathon on March 5, 2026, for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S, following a free preview weekend. The launch is generating buzz and controversy linked to streamer Dr Disrespect rumors, testing Bungie's ability to revive the multiplayer shooter genre.[1]

items.title: EA Cuts Jobs at Full Circle Studio
items.summary: Electronic Arts is laying off an undisclosed number of employees at its Burnaby, BC-based Full Circle studio as part of a 'reshaping' effort. Affected workers, including environment artists, have shared their status on LinkedIn.[2]

items.title: GameSquare Expands Partnership with Capcom for Resident Evil Requiem Launch
items.summary: GameSquare has expanded its partnership with Capcom to support the global launch of Resident Evil Requiem. The announcement was made on February 26, 2026.[3]

items.title: Splash Damage Acquires SCUM Developer Gamepires from Jagex
items.summary: UK studio Splash Damage has acquired Croatian open-world survival game developer Gamepires, known for SCUM, from Jagex for an undisclosed amount. This merger aims to bolster co-development capabilities.[6]

items.title: Xsolla Sponsors Türkiye Game Market Report 2025
items.summary: Xsolla announced its sponsorship of the Türkiye Game Market Report 2025 on February 26, 2026, highlighting growth in the regional gaming sector.[7]

items.title: DraftKings Announces Job Cuts in Restructuring
items.summary: Sports betting and gaming company DraftKings is cutting jobs to reduce general and administrative costs as part of a company reorganization.[8]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: EY Canada Secures Patent for Hybrid Quantum-Classical Computing Innovation
items.summary: EY Canada has patented a new quantum technology that integrates quantum operators with classical systems to enhance enterprise decision-making in complex areas like supply chains and operations. The innovation, developed by a multidisciplinary team, aims to improve decision quality under uncertainty without replacing traditional computing.

items.title: IonQ Deploys One of Europe's Largest Operational Quantum Key Distribution Networks in Romania
items.summary: IonQ has successfully launched Romania's National Quantum Communication Infrastructure, establishing one of the largest terrestrial Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) networks in Europe for secure communications.
